question: Does OncoTarget-1 improve survival overall versus standard of care?
Which cancers does OncoTarget-1 help — and which does it not?
Which patient subgroup benefits most from OncoTarget-1?
Within breast cancer, how does the responder molecular subtype compare to other breast patients?
For which cancers is OncoTarget-1 not indicated (no benefit or worse outcomes)?
What is the 24-month survival rate by treatment arm for ovarian and prostate cancer?
Does OncoTarget-1 improve survival overall?
sql: SELECT treatment_arm, patients, survival_pct FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_hls_genomics_v2.arm_summary ORDER BY treatment_arm

question: Which cancers does OncoTarget-1 help, and which not?
sql:
SELECT tissue, survival_lift, patients,
  CASE WHEN survival_lift>=3 THEN 'Benefits' WHEN survival_lift<=-3 THEN 'Not indicated' ELSE 'No effect' END AS verdict
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_hls_genomics_v2.lift_by_site ORDER BY survival_lift DESC

question: Survival by arm for ovarian and prostate cancer
sql:
SELECT tissue, treatment_arm, patients, survival_pct
FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_hls_genomics_v2.survival_by_arm
WHERE tissue IN ('Ovary','Prostate gland') ORDER BY tissue, treatment_arm

question: Survival curve over time by arm
sql: SELECT month, treatment_arm, pct_surviving FROM main.dbdemos_aibi_hls_genomics_v2.survival_curve ORDER BY month, treatment_arm

content:
THE STORY. This is real-world evidence (RWE) for a new targeted cancer therapy called OncoTarget-1, evaluated against standard of care across a real ~10,000-patient oncology cohort (TCGA-style genomics: cancer site, gene-expression UMAP coordinates, demographics). Each patient is in one of two treatment arms: 'OncoTarget-1' or 'Standard of care'. survived_24mo = survived 24 months; months_survived = months survived (survivors censored at 24).
THE KEY INSIGHT the dashboard surfaces: the drug works, but UNEVENLY. Overall survival is higher for treated patients (~80% vs ~75%), BUT the benefit varies wildly by cancer: a large lift in melanoma (Skin), Cerebrum, Bladder and Breast; little/no effect in lung, brain, thyroid; and it is NOT INDICATED for Ovary and Prostate, where treated patients do WORSE (negative lift — toxicity outweighs benefit). lift_by_site.survival_lift is the treated-minus-standard survival gap per cancer (positive=helps, negative=harms).
THE 'WHY' / DEEP-DIVE: the responders are defined by a MOLECULAR signature, not an organ. Within breast cancer there is a responder molecular subtype (a distinct gene-expression UMAP cluster, patient_cohort.responder_subtype = TRUE). In that subtype OncoTarget-1 lifts 24-month survival to ~99% vs ~89% for standard of care — far more than in other breast patients. breast_deepdive splits breast into 'Responder subtype' vs 'Other breast' by arm.
WHICH TABLE TO USE: arm_summary = overall survival by treatment arm. lift_by_site = survival lift per cancer site (the 'where does it help/harm' question). survival_by_arm = survival % per cancer site x arm. breast_deepdive = the responder-subtype deep-dive. survival_curve = % surviving by month (0-24) per arm, for Kaplan-Meier-style curves. patient_cohort = patient-level table (case_id, primary_diagnosis, tissue, gender, race, age, c1/c2 UMAP coords, treatment_arm, responder_subtype, survived_24mo, months_survived) for any custom slice.
When asked 'which subgroup benefits most' answer: the breast-cancer responder molecular subtype (use breast_deepdive). When asked 'where should we use / not use the drug' use lift_by_site. Compute survival rate as AVG(CASE WHEN survived_24mo THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) when working from patient_cohort. Don't answer questions unrelated to this oncology RWE analysis.
